paper_title: A Simple Theory of Economic Development at the Extensive Industry Margin
research_answer: It's quite rare. Across 38,586 country-industry observations where a country lacked a revealed comparative advantage (RCA > 1) in a given industry, new entry occurred in only about 5% of cases in any given period. This 5% baseline — or unconditional probability — is the benchmark against which proximity effects are measured. The fact that occupational distance can swing the probability from 2% (maximum distance) to 8% (zero distance) means that being capability-ready quadruples the chances of crossing even this relatively low baseline threshold.
supporting_quotes: In about 5% of these observations, we record the appearance of a new country-industry combination.
The unconditional probability (5%, the mean of a^i_{c,t}).
